operating_state: Total revenues increased to $65.4 billion from $36.1 billion prior year, driven by higher crude oil and natural gas realizations across upstream and downstream segments. Upstream earnings reached $8.6 billion versus $3.2 billion, with international operations outperforming domestic. Downstream earnings increased to $3.5 billion from $839 million, reflecting improved refining margins and favorable foreign exchange effects. Free cash flow more than doubled to $16.7 billion in the first six months. Net oil-equivalent production decreased 5% to 2.98 million barrels per day primarily due to contract expirations in Thailand and Indonesia.
cost_structure: Purchased crude oil and products costs doubled to $40.0 billion from $20.6 billion, reflecting higher commodity prices and volumes. Operating expenses increased due to early contract termination charges at Sabine Pass and higher transportation costs, partially offset by lower employee benefit expenses. Capital and exploratory expenditures increased to $6.7 billion in the first six months from $5.3 billion, with upstream investments representing 79% of total spending. Depreciation decreased to $3.7 billion from $4.5 billion due to lower rates and reduced production volumes.
material_events: Completed acquisition of Renewable Energy Group for $3.15 billion in June 2022, adding 11 biorefineries and establishing Chevron as a leading U.S. renewable fuels producer. Sanctioned the Ballymore deepwater Gulf of Mexico project and formed Bunge Chevron Ag Renewables joint venture for renewable fuel feedstock development. Executed $15.2 billion in share repurchases during the quarter at an average price of $164.84 per share and increased quarterly dividend to $1.42 per share.
macro_exposures: Commodity derivative losses totaled $1.2 billion in the first six months compared to $601 million in 2021, reflecting increased market volatility. CPC pipeline operations faced temporary disruption from weather-related damage to offshore loading moorings in March, curtailing TCO production to 70% capacity until April repairs. Supply chain disruptions continue limiting availability of industry inputs with longer lead times and inflationary pressures.
forward_outlook: Management increased annual share repurchase guidance to $15 billion with $3.75 billion planned for Q3 2022, maintaining $14.4 billion in remaining authorization. The company targets growth in traditional oil and gas business alongside lower carbon businesses in renewable fuels, hydrogen, and carbon capture. Management maintains flexibility to modify capital spending, discontinue share repurchases, and increase borrowings during extended low commodity price periods while preserving dividend payments.
uncertainty_profile: Climate change litigation continues from 21 separate lawsuits filed by U.S. cities, counties, states and trade groups seeking damages related to fossil fuel production. Ecuador-related arbitration proceedings continue to determine compensation owed following tribunal findings of fraudulent judgment. Louisiana coastal erosion lawsuits involving 39 cases present unprecedented legal theories with uncertain damage scope. Expected credit loss allowance increased to $780 million with majority relating to non-trade receivables including government joint venture partners.
